messages.content:
请口语化，我是讲报告：In the future, HIAF will continue to build EicC, CHNS and other devices based on track detectors, which are very important for the performance characterization of track detectors. Therefore, a detector system for heavy ion track positioning is very necessary. So we are developing heavy ion beam telescopes. It is designed based on the Topmetal-M pixel sensor and is expected to achieve a positioning accuracy of 9 microns. The system has 8 front-end boards, one back-end board, 6 detection layers, 2 trigger layers, and DAQ software. The front-end and back-end firmware design has been completed.
On the other hand, we also completed the establishment of the cluster model of the Topmetal sensor based on Geant4, and the simulation results are consistent with the experiments. Based on this, we continue to develop the ion track positioning algorithm and are currently iterating.
This is my poster, welcome to communicate with me.

嗨，大家好！今天，我想和大家聊聊我们在未来准备做的一些超酷的事情。我们要继续建设HIAF的EicC、CHNS等装置，这些都是基于径迹探测器的。讲真，径迹探测器的性能表征离不开这些装置的，所以说，搞个重离子径迹定位的探测器系统是相当有必要的。

接下来的事情更酷：我们正在研发一种重离子束望远镜。这货是基于Topmetal-M像素传感器设计的，预计定位精度能达到9微米级别，是不是很厉害？这套系统组成还挺复杂，有8块前端板，一块后端板，6层探测层，2层触发层，还有个数据获取软件。

至于前端和后端的固件设计，我们已经搞定了。另一方面，我们也基于Geant4完成了Topmetal传感器的簇模型建立，模拟结果和实验差不多，这可是个好消息。基于这个，我们持续在开发改进离子径迹定位算法，现在还在不断迭代中。

这就是我的报告内容，欢迎大家和我交流哦！

使用英语：In the future, HIAF will continue to build EicC, CHNS and other devices based on track detectors, which are very important for the performance characterization of track detectors. Therefore, a detector system for heavy ion track positioning is very necessary. So we are developing heavy ion beam telescopes. It is designed based on the Topmetal-M pixel sensor and is expected to achieve a positioning accuracy of 9 microns. The system has 8 front-end boards, one back-end board, 6 detection layers, 2 trigger layers, and DAQ software. The front-end and back-end firmware design has been completed.
On the other hand, we also completed the establishment of the cluster model of the Topmetal sensor based on Geant4, and the simulation results are consistent with the experiments. Based on this, we continue to develop the ion track positioning algorithm and are currently iterating.
This is my poster, welcome to communicate with me.

Hey everyone! Today, I'm really excited to talk to you about what we're looking forward to doing in the future. We're going to continue working on building devices like EicC, CHNS, and others at HIAF, all of which rely on track detectors. Honestly, these devices are super important for figuring out how well track detectors perform. So, having a system that can accurately position heavy ion tracks? Absolutely essential.

Here's where it gets even cooler: we're in the process of developing heavy ion beam telescopes. These bad boys are designed around the Topmetal-M pixel sensor, and we're aiming for a positioning accuracy of about 9 microns – pretty impressive, right? This system is kind of a big deal, with 8 front-end boards, one back-end board, 6 detection layers, 2 trigger layers, and DAQ software to boot.

We've already nailed the design for both the front-end and back-end firmware. On another note, we've also managed to set up a cluster model for the Topmetal sensor using Geant4, and guess what? The simulation results line up with our experiments. With that under our belts, we're pushing forward to further develop and iterate the ion track positioning algorithm.

So, this is what I've got for you on my poster. I'm looking forward to chatting and exchanging ideas with you all!
再书面一点

Greetings,

I am pleased to present future prospects regarding the development and enhancement of tracking detectors at HIAF, specifically focusing on the ongoing initiatives involving EicC, CHNS, and comparable devices. These advancements are pivotal for the nuanced characterization and performance assessment of track detectors, underscoring the necessity for a dedicated detector system tailored for heavy ion track positioning.

In response to this exigency, we are at the forefront of developing an innovative heavy ion beam telescope. This cutting-edge instrument is predicated on the implementation of the Topmetal-M pixel sensor, aiming to achieve an unprecedented positioning accuracy of 9 microns. The configuration of this system is robust, comprising 8 front-end boards, a singular back-end board, alongside 6 detection layers, 2 trigger layers, and sophisticated DAQ software. I am pleased to report that the firmware design for both the front-end and back-end has been successfully completed.

Moreover, we have achieved significant progress in the emulation of the Topmetal sensor's cluster model utilizing Geant4, with simulation results that consistently align with empirical data. This milestone facilitates the continuous refinement and iteration of our ion track positioning algorithm.

I extend an invitation for discussions and exchange of insights regarding this poster presentation. Your feedback and queries are highly appreciated.

Thank you.

一些口语，不要改变原文意思：In the future, HIAF will continue to build EicC, CHNS and other devices based on track detectors, which are very important for the performance characterization of track detectors. Therefore, a detector system for heavy ion track positioning is very necessary. So we are developing heavy ion beam telescopes. It is designed based on the Topmetal-M pixel sensor and is expected to achieve a positioning accuracy of 9 microns. The system has 8 front-end boards, one back-end board, 6 detection layers, 2 trigger layers, and DAQ software. The front-end and back-end firmware design has been completed.
On the other hand, we also completed the establishment of the cluster model of the Topmetal sensor based on Geant4, and the simulation results are consistent with the experiments. Based on this, we continue to develop the ion track positioning algorithm and are currently iterating.
This is my poster, welcome to communicate with me.

Hey there,

Just wanted to share some cool stuff we're doing in the future at HIAF. We're gonna keep on building some awesome devices like EicC, CHNS, and others that depend on track detectors. These are super crucial for checking out how well our track detectors are performing. So, yeah, it's pretty clear we really need a solid detector system for pinpointing where heavy ion tracks are.

That's why we're putting together these heavy ion beam telescopes. They're based on this nifty Topmetal-M pixel sensor and we're aiming for them to be super precise, like, hitting a positioning accuracy of 9 microns. The setup's got 8 front-end boards, one back-end board, 6 detection layers, 2 trigger layers, and some DAQ software. Plus, we've already wrapped up the design work for both the front-end and back-end firmware.

Oh, and there's more. We've also got this cluster model of the Topmetal sensor up and running with Geant4, and the cool part is, the simulation's lining up pretty well with what we're seeing in the real-world tests. So, we're pushing ahead, tweaking and improving our ion track positioning algorithm as we go.

This is what I've been working on. If you wanna chat or have any questions, feel free to hit me up.
未来在HIAF将继续建设基于径迹探测器的EicC，CHNS等装置，对于径迹探测器的性能表征是非常重要的。
在未来，HIAF将会继续推进EicC、CHNS等基于径迹探测器的装置的建设，这对于评估和理解径迹探测器的性能至关重要。
英语，

In the future, HIAF will continue the development of devices such as EicC and CHNS, which are based on track detectors. This is extremely important for the performance characterization of track detectors.
不要太书面
Going forward, HIAF will keep on rolling out devices like EicC and CHNS that rely on track detectors. It's really crucial for figuring out how well these track detectors work.
口语化

So, in the future, HIAF is going to keep building stuff like EicC and CHNS, which all use track detectors. It's super important because it helps us understand how good these track detectors actually are.
未来在HIAF将继续建设EicC，CHNS等装置非常依赖于内径迹探测器，那么内径迹探测器的性能表征是非常重要的。

Looking ahead, HIAF is going to keep developing gadgets like EicC and CHNS, which really lean on using track detectors inside. So, getting a handle on how well these internal track detectors perform is pretty key.
未来在HIAF将继续建设EicC，CHNS等装置非常依赖于径迹探测器，那么径迹探测器的性能表征是非常重要的。

Moving forward, HIAF plans to keep up with building stuff like EicC and CHNS, and these projects really depend on track detectors. So, understanding how well these track detectors work is super important.
是性能刻度非常重要
Moving forward, HIAF plans to continue with projects like EicC and CHNS, which heavily rely on track detectors. So, accurately calibrating the performance of these track detectors is super crucial.
这是我的海报，欢迎与我交流。
This is my poster; I'd love to chat with you about it.
换种表达
Here's my poster - feel free to strike up a conversation with me about it.
